title: Ancient Civilizations: Early Ideas of Freedom
description: This coloring page introduces young students to concepts of democracy and citizenship from ancient civilizations, which later influenced many modern nations including America.
instructions: Color each part of the picture using your favorite colors. As you color, think about how people in ancient times lived and worked together.

content.image: A simple outline drawing showing the Parthenon of Ancient Greece with several children in togas gathered in front, appearing to be voting or making decisions together. The scene includes simple architectural elements and symbols of early democracy.

elements.name: Parthenon
elements.description: This was an important building in Ancient Greece where people gathered

elements.name: Children
elements.description: These represent citizens participating in early democracy

elements.name: Voting area
elements.description: A place where people shared their ideas and made decisions together

elements.name: Olive tree
elements.description: A symbol of peace and prosperity in ancient times

learningFacts: Ancient Greece had one of the world's first democracies
In Athens, citizens would gather to vote on important decisions
Ancient civilizations created many ideas about governing that we still use today
People in ancient times worked together to build communities
